message:
Add null defaults for UDO field parameters (#5753)

## Summary

Enable user-defined operators (UDOs) in Tenzir packages to have
field-type parameters with null as a default value. This allows package
authors to create operators with optional field selector arguments that
default to null when not provided.

Key features:
- Field parameters can only default to `null` (non-null defaults are
rejected with clear errors)
- Callers can omit field arguments to use the null default
- Explicitly passing `null` to a field parameter is invalid
- 11 comprehensive test cases covering happy paths and error conditions

## Changes

- **libtenzir/src/package.cpp**: Validates that field-type parameters
can only have `null` as their default value
- **libtenzir/src/tql2/user_defined_operator.cpp**: Handles null
defaults during operator instantiation and validates explicitly provided
field values
- **Test cases**: Added 8 new test files with passing and failing
scenarios

## Test plan

- All existing tests pass
- New test cases validate:
  - Field parameters defaulting to null
  - Field parameters with provided values
  - Error handling for non-null defaults on field parameters
  - Error handling for explicitly passing null to field parameters

## Documentation PR

- tenzir/docs#204

<sub>resolves TNZ-213</sub>

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

files.patch:
@@ -78,6 +78,7 @@ Common options:
 - `--passthrough`: Generate output in terminal instead (good on first run)
 - `--update`: Update reference outputs after validating changes
 - `--debug`: Show detailed test information
+- `--match`: Filter test paths with glob expression
 
 Reference documentation: https://docs.tenzir.com/reference/test-framework.md

@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+---
+title: Optional field parameters for user-defined operators
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 5753
+created: 2026-02-12T17:49:08.631507Z
+---
+
+User-defined operators in packages can now declare optional field-type parameters with `null` as the default value. This allows operators to accept field selectors that are not required to be provided.
+
+When a field parameter is declared with `type: field` and `default: null`, you can omit the argument when calling the operator, and the parameter will receive a `null` value instead. You can then check whether a field was provided by comparing the parameter to `null` within the operator definition.
+
+Example:
+
+In your package's operator definition, declare an optional field parameter:
+
+```yaml
+args:
+  named:
+    - name: selector
+      type: field
+      default: null
+```
+
+In the operator implementation, check if the field was provided:
+
+```tql
+set result = if $selector != null then "field provided" else "field omitted"
+```
+
+When calling the operator, the field argument becomes optional:
+
+```tql
+my_operator                    # field is null
+my_operator selector=x.y       # field is x.y
+```
+
+Only `null` is allowed as the default value for field parameters. Non-null defaults are rejected with an error during package loading.

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+---
+description: "Invalid non-null default for a field parameter"
+args:
+  named:
+    - name: field
+      type: field
+      default: this.input
+---
+set marker = "should not load"

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+id: argspkg_field_default_failing
+name: Field Default Failing Package
+description: Package with invalid non-null field defaults.

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+description: "Mark whether an optional field argument was provided"
+args:
+  named:
+    - name: field
+      type: field
+      default: null
+      description: "Optional field selector"
+---
+set field_provided = $field != null

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+error: default value for field parameter `field` must be `null`

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+error: expected a selector
+ --> tests/packages/udo-args/udo-args-field-default-null-explicit-null-invalid.tql:6:41
+  |
+6 | argspkg::optional_field_indicator field=null
+  |                                         ^^^^ 
+  |
+  = usage: argspkg::optional_field_indicator [field=field]
+  = note: parameters:
+            named:
+              name   type   default  description
+              field  field  ~        Optional field selector
